title: euonymus hamiltonianus subsp. sieboldianus
intro: Euonymus hamiltonianus subsp. sieboldianus, also known as Siebold's spindle tree, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its distinctive red or orange seeds and compound leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities. Traditional herbal preparations of Siebold's spindle tree include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, such as hypertension, fever, and respiratory infections. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
